---
title: "shinygen"
subtitle: "Generate, evaluate, and refine Shiny apps using LLM agents in Docker sandboxes"
jupyter: shinygen
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: flatly
      dark: darkly
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-copy: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    smooth-scroll: true
    anchor-sections: true
    fontsize: 1.05em
    mainfont: "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif"
    highlight-style: github
    css: styles.css
---

::: {.lead}
**shinygen** turns a text prompt into a production-quality [Shiny](https://shiny.posit.co) dashboard ‚Äî generated, screenshot-tested, and quality-scored ‚Äî all inside an isolated Docker sandbox.
:::

---

## {{< fa bolt >}} Quick Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/karangathe/shinygen.git
cd shinygen
pip install -e .
```

For screenshot-based evaluation, install the optional extra:

```bash
pip install -e ".[screenshot]"
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Docker images with Shiny, Playwright, and Chromium are pulled automatically on first run.
:::

---

## {{< fa diagram-project >}} Architecture

The current loop is **generate inside a fresh sandbox ‚Üí optionally self-evaluate visually ‚Üí optionally judge externally ‚Üí refine**.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 9
flowchart TD
  A[User Prompt + flags] --> B[shinygen CLI / API]
    B --> C["Pre-flight checks<br/>Docker + API key"]
    C --> D["Load framework defaults,<br/>data files, and skills"]
  D --> E[Iteration loop]

  subgraph F[Fresh Docker sandbox per iteration]
    direction TB
        G["Stage Docker context<br/>+ Inspect AI task"]
    H[LLM Agent]
    I[Generate app.py / app.R]
    J{--screenshot?}
        K["Run app on :8000<br/>inside sandbox"]
        L["screenshot_helper.py<br/>waits 7s and captures screenshot.png"]
        M["Agent reviews screenshot<br/>and refines in sandbox"]
        N["Scorer copies project files<br/>to results volume"]
    O[Eval log + usage rows]

    E --> G --> H --> I --> J
    J -- Yes --> K --> L --> M --> N
    J -- No --> N
    N --> O
  end

    O --> P["Extract code from results volume<br/>or eval log"]
    O --> Q["Copy agent_last_screenshot.png<br/>from results or eval attachment"]
  P --> R{--judge-model?}
  R -- Yes --> S[Run extracted app on host temp dir]
  S --> T[Host Playwright screenshot]
  T --> U[External LLM judge]
  U --> V{Score ‚â• threshold?}
    V -- No --> W["Refinement prompt includes<br/>judge feedback + previous code"]
  W --> E
  V -- Yes --> X[Output directory]
  R -- No --> X

  Q --> X

  style A fill:#6c5ce7,color:#fff,stroke:none
  style B fill:#0984e3,color:#fff,stroke:none
  style C fill:#74b9ff,color:#fff,stroke:none
  style D fill:#636e72,color:#fff,stroke:none
  style F fill:#f8f9fa,stroke:#dee2e6,stroke-width:2px
  style H fill:#00b894,color:#fff,stroke:none
  style I fill:#fdcb6e,color:#2d3436,stroke:none
  style Q fill:#00cec9,color:#fff,stroke:none
  style U fill:#e17055,color:#fff,stroke:none
  style X fill:#00b894,color:#fff,stroke:none
```

### How it works

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Step-by-step walkthrough (click to expand)

1. **You run a command** ‚Äî provide a prompt, pick a model, optionally enable `--screenshot` and `--judge-model`.
2. **Pre-flight + setup** ‚Äî shinygen verifies Docker/API access, resolves the framework and model, and loads bundled skills, custom skills, and any data files.
3. **Fresh sandbox generation** ‚Äî each iteration stages a fresh Docker sandbox, installs agent skills in the agent's native home, and asks Claude Code or Codex CLI to generate `app.py` or `app.R`.
4. **Visual self-evaluation** ‚Äî when screenshot mode is enabled, the agent runs the app inside the sandbox, uses `screenshot_helper.py`, waits 7 seconds before capture, and refines visually before submission.
5. **Extraction + optional external judge** ‚Äî shinygen extracts the app from the results volume or eval log, preserves the final in-agent screenshot as `agent_last_screenshot.png`, and, if `--judge-model` is set, runs the extracted app on the host for a separate Playwright screenshot and external LLM score. Failed scores feed a refinement prompt that includes both judge feedback and the previous code.
6. **Final output** ‚Äî the final app, screenshots, eval logs, and structured run summary are written to your output directory.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Iteration by Feedback

With `max_iterations > 1`, each round runs in a fresh sandbox. The refinement prompt carries forward judge feedback **and** the previous code, so the agent can improve incrementally rather than starting from scratch.

**Original prompt (iteration 1):**

```text
Create a Shiny dashboard for clinical trials with filters, a trend chart, and a summary table.
```

**Refinement prompt (iteration 2):**

```text
Create a Shiny dashboard for clinical trials with filters, a trend chart, and a summary table.

--- PREVIOUS CODE ---
<the full app.py from the previous iteration>

--- REFINEMENT (iteration 1) ---

A previous version of this app was evaluated and received the following scores.
Please improve the app to address the feedback:

  - Functionality: 7.0/10 ‚Äî Filters work as expected.
  - Design: 5.5/10 ‚Äî Layout is cramped and spacing is inconsistent.
  - Code Quality: 6.0/10 ‚Äî Repeated logic in multiple places.
  - Ux: 6.2/10 ‚Äî Table readability is weak.

Focus on improving the lowest-scoring areas while maintaining what already works well.
Produce the complete, improved app file.
```
:::

---

## {{< fa boxes-stacked >}} What You Get

```
my-dashboard/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app.py                  # Or app.R for Shiny for R
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ data.csv                # Your data file (if provided)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ screenshot.png          # Host-side screenshot used by the external judge
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ agent_last_screenshot.png
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ eval_logs/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ *.eval
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ run_summary.json        # Structured score, usage, and artifact metadata
```

The CLI prints a results summary:

```
Score: 8.25 / 10.00 (after 2 iterations)
Time:  45.2s total (38.1s generate, 7.1s judge)
Tokens: 12,340 input / 3,210 output
Cost:  $0.1842
```

---

## {{< fa star >}} Features

::: {.feature-grid}

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa robot >}} Multiple LLM Agents
Claude Code (Anthropic) and Codex CLI (OpenAI) ‚Äî pick the model that fits your budget and quality needs.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa brands docker >}} Docker Sandboxes
Every generation runs in an isolated container via [Inspect AI](https://inspect.ai-safety-institute.org.uk/). No side effects on your host.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa brands python >}} {{< fa brands r-project >}} R and Python
Generate Shiny for Python (`app.py`) or Shiny for R (`app.R`) from the same CLI.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa camera-retro >}} Visual Self-Evaluation
The agent takes Playwright screenshots *inside* the sandbox, reviews them, and self-corrects layout/styling issues.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa scale-balanced >}} External LLM Judge
A separate model scores apps on functionality, design, code quality, and UX ‚Äî then triggers refinement if needed.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa arrows-rotate >}} Iterative Refinement
Automatically re-generate until the quality threshold is met, up to `--max-iterations`.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa book >}} Skills Injection
Pass custom skill files into the agent sandbox to control coding style, component choices, and best practices.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa globe >}} Web Fetch
Enabled by default. Allows the agent to search the web during generation for up-to-date API docs and examples. Use `--no-web-fetch` to disable.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
#### {{< fa coins >}} Cost & Time Tracking
Token usage, dollar costs, and timing breakdowns reported per run.
:::

:::

---

## {{< fa terminal >}} Quick Start

### CLI

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Basic

```bash
# Generate a Shiny for Python app with Claude Sonnet
shinygen generate \
    --prompt "Create a sales dashboard with filters by region and product category" \
    --model claude-sonnet \
    --output ./my-dashboard
```

#### With Screenshot + Judge

```bash
# Screenshot-based quality evaluation and iteration
shinygen generate \
    --prompt "Create a clinical trials dashboard" \
    --model claude-opus \
    --output ./trials-app \
    --screenshot \
    --judge-model claude-sonnet \
    --max-iterations 5
```

#### Shiny for R

```bash
shinygen generate \
    --prompt "Create an interactive data explorer" \
    --model claude-sonnet \
    --framework shiny-r \
    --output ./r-app
```

#### With Data + Skills

```bash
shinygen generate \
    --prompt "Build a dashboard for this dataset" \
    --model gpt54 \
    --output ./my-app \
    --skills-dir ./my-skills/ \
    --csv-file ./sales.csv
```

`web_fetch` is enabled by default. Use `--no-web-fetch` to disable web search.

:::

### Python API

```python
import shinygen

result = shinygen.generate(
    prompt="Create a sales dashboard with regional filters",
    model="claude-sonnet",
    output_dir="./my-dashboard",
    framework="shiny-python",
    data_csv="./sales.csv",
    screenshot=True,
    judge_model="claude-sonnet",
    max_iterations=5,
)

print(result.app_dir)       # ./my-dashboard
print(result.score)          # 4.5
print(result.iterations)    # 2
```

### Batch Generation

Run the **same prompt across multiple models** (or different prompts with different settings) in a single command. Each job gets its own output directory.

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### CLI

Create a JSON config file. Each object is one job ‚Äî same keys as the Python API. You can mix models, frameworks, and prompts freely.

```{.json filename="batch.json"}
[
  {
    "prompt": "Create a clinical trials dashboard with enrollment trends and status filters",
    "model": "claude-sonnet",
    "output_dir": "./batch/sonnet",
    "csv_file": "./test_data_csv_files/trials_short_50.csv",
    "screenshot": true,
        "judge_model": "openai/gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17",
    "max_iterations": 3
  },
  { "model": "gpt54",       "output_dir": "./batch/gpt54", "..." : "same prompt/settings" },
  { "model": "claude-opus",  "output_dir": "./batch/opus",  "..." : "same prompt/settings" }
]
```

Then run:

```bash
shinygen batch --config batch.json
```

Output:

```
Starting batch run with 3 job(s)...

Batch complete: 3 succeeded, 0 failed
  [job 1] ./batch/sonnet  score=8.50  iterations=2  passed=True
  [job 2] ./batch/gpt54   score=7.25  iterations=3  passed=True
  [job 3] ./batch/opus    score=9.00  iterations=1  passed=True
```

#### Python API

```python
import shinygen

results = shinygen.batch([
    {
        "prompt": "Clinical trials dashboard with filters and charts",
        "model": "claude-sonnet",
        "output_dir": "./batch/sonnet",
        "data_csv": "./test_data_csv_files/trials_short_50.csv",
        "screenshot": True,
        "judge_model": "openai/gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17",
    },
    # Add more jobs with different models/prompts...
])

print(f"{results.succeeded} succeeded, {results.failed} failed")
for r in results.results:
    print(f"  {r.app_dir}  score={r.score:.2f}")
```

:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Batch tips

- Jobs run **sequentially** (no Docker resource contention).
- A failed job doesn't stop the rest.
- Use different `output_dir` values per job.
- Relative paths resolve from the config file's directory.
:::

---

## {{< fa microchip >}} Supported Models

```{python}
#| echo: false
from great_tables import GT, md
import polars as pl

models = pl.DataFrame({
    "Alias": ["`claude-opus`", "`claude-sonnet`", "`gpt54`", "`gpt54-mini`", "`codex-gpt53`"],
    "Agent": ["Claude Code", "Claude Code", "Codex CLI", "Codex CLI", "Codex CLI"],
    "Model ID": ["`claude-opus-4-7`", "`claude-sonnet-4-6`", "`gpt-5.4`", "`gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17`", "`gpt-5.3-codex`"],
    "Provider": ["Anthropic", "Anthropic", "OpenAI", "OpenAI", "OpenAI"],
})

(
    GT(models)
    .tab_header(title="Supported Models")
    .fmt_markdown(columns=["Alias", "Model ID"])
    .cols_align(align="left")
    .opt_stylize(style=1, color="blue")
)
```

---

## {{< fa chart-bar >}} Cost Comparison

Per 1M tokens. Actual cost per dashboard is typically **$0.01‚Äì$0.30** depending on model, prompt complexity, and iterations.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from great_tables import GT, md
import polars as pl

costs = pl.DataFrame({
    "Model": ["`claude-opus`", "`claude-sonnet`", "`gpt54`", "`gpt54-mini`", "`codex-gpt53`"],
    "Input": [5.00, 3.00, 2.50, 0.75, 1.75],
    "Output": [25.00, 15.00, 15.00, 4.50, 14.00],
})

(
    GT(costs)
    .tab_header(
        title="Cost per 1M Tokens",
        subtitle="USD pricing by model",
    )
    .fmt_markdown(columns="Model")
    .fmt_currency(columns=["Input", "Output"], currency="USD")
    .cols_label(Input="Input ($/1M tokens)", Output="Output ($/1M tokens)")
    .cols_align(align="right", columns=["Input", "Output"])
    .cols_align(align="left", columns="Model")
    .data_color(
        columns="Output",
        palette=["#d4edda", "#f8d7da"],
    )
    .opt_stylize(style=1, color="blue")
)
```

---

## {{< fa database >}} Data Inputs

```{python}
#| echo: false
from great_tables import GT, md
import polars as pl

data_inputs = pl.DataFrame({
    "Option": ["Single CSV", "Multiple files"],
    "CLI": ["`--csv-file`", "`--data-file` (repeatable)"],
    "Python API": ["`data_csv`", "`data_files`"],
    "Notes": ["Convenience shorthand", "Any file type"],
})

(
    GT(data_inputs)
    .tab_header(title="Data Input Options")
    .fmt_markdown(columns=["CLI", "Python API"])
    .cols_align(align="left")
    .opt_stylize(style=1, color="blue")
    .tab_source_note(md("If both provide the same filename, `--csv-file` takes precedence."))
)
```

---

## {{< fa list-check >}} Requirements

```{python}
#| echo: false
from great_tables import GT, md
import polars as pl

reqs = pl.DataFrame({
    "Requirement": ["Python", "Docker", "API Key"],
    "Details": ["3.10+", "Running daemon", "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY"],
})

(
    GT(reqs)
    .tab_header(title="Prerequisites")
    .cols_align(align="left")
    .opt_stylize(style=1, color="blue")
    .tab_source_note(
        md("`export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='sk-ant-...'` or `export OPENAI_API_KEY='sk-...'`")
    )
)
```

---

## {{< fa scale-balanced >}} License

[MIT](LICENSE)
